figures.caption: Fig. 4. Qualitative reconstruction fidelity with ADaCoRe. For each dataset, we show a representative segment from the first few EEG channels (rows) and three traces per channel (columns): original waveform, waveform reconstructed from the compressed representation, and pointwise reconstruction error (Recovered − Original). The title of each original panel reports three agreement measures between the original signal x and its reconstruction x̃: r (Pearson correlation over time), SNR in dB (signal power vs. reconstruction error power), and PSDcos (cosine similarity between Welch power spectra). Higher r, SNR, and PSDcos indicate better preservation of waveform shape, amplitude, and frequency content.
TABLE II ABLATION OF COMPRESSION, RECONSTRUCTION, AND PROTOTYPE-CONFIDENCE SELECTION (PCS) ON FACED. THE BUFFER SIZE FOR PCS IS SET TO 135 FULL SAMPLES.

Fig. 1. UICL. A source-pretrained model M0 adapts to a sequence of unlabeled subject streams {U(t)}Tt=1 using a memory-bounded replay buffer, producing Mt after subject t. Plasticity and stability are measured on the current and future subjects, respectively.
Fig. 5. FACED sensitivity for keyframe hyperparameters. Left: ϕ ∈ [0.07, 0.15] with ρ=0.5 s. Right: ρ ∈ [0.25, 1.25] s with ϕ=0.1.

Fig. 2. Overview of ADaCoRe. Saliency detection (§IV-A) selects protected indices P . Non-protected regions are compressed via rational polyphase resampling (§IV-B). Replay uses adjoint reconstruction with verbatim overwrite of protected samples (§IV-C). Exemplar maintenance respects class prototypes and pseudo-label confidence (§IV-D).
